indication: Exertional dyspnea and fatigue, evaluate for ischemia and wall motion abnormalities in setting of known coronary artery disease
technique: Treadmill stress echocardiography using the Bruce protocol. Resting and post-exercise 2D and Doppler echocardiographic images were obtained in standard views.

protocol.symptoms: Moderate dyspnea at peak exercise, no chest pain
protocol.ecg: Baseline: Atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response. Stress: No diagnostic ST changes. Atrial fibrillation with rate to 102 bpm.

restingImages.leftVentricle: Mildly dilated (LVIDd 5.8 cm). Mild global hypokinesis. Hypokinesis of the inferior and inferolateral walls.
restingImages.valves: Mild mitral regurgitation. Aortic valve trileaflet with mild sclerosis. Trace tricuspid regurgitation.

stressImages.wallMotion: Worsening hypokinesis of the inferior and inferolateral walls at peak stress. New hypokinesis of the lateral wall. Anterior and anteroseptal walls augmented normally.
stressImages.newAbnormalities: Stress-induced wall motion abnormality in the lateral wall, suggesting inducible ischemia in the LCx territory.

doppler.mitralRegurgitation: Mild-to-moderate at rest, increasing to moderate with stress.
doppler.rvsp: 35 mmHg at rest, 48 mmHg at peak stress.

impression: Mildly dilated left ventricle with mildly-to-moderately reduced systolic function at rest (EF 35-40%), worsening to 30-35% at peak stress.
Resting wall motion abnormalities in the inferior and inferolateral walls, consistent with prior infarction in the RCA territory.
Stress-induced wall motion abnormality in the lateral wall, suggesting inducible ischemia in the left circumflex territory.
Exercise capacity is reduced (5 minutes 42 seconds, Stage 2, 69% age-predicted maximum heart rate).
Mild-to-moderate mitral regurgitation worsening with exercise.
Findings are consistent with ischemic cardiomyopathy with inducible ischemia.
recommendations: Recommend nuclear myocardial perfusion imaging for further assessment of ischemic burden
Optimize guideline-directed medical therapy for heart failure
Consider cardiology consultation regarding coronary revascularization
comparison: No prior stress echocardiogram available for comparison.
